raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Barclays improper handling of an unauthorized fraudulent transaction on my credit card account. 

The disputed transaction is an XXXX charge in the amount of {$68.00} dated XX/XX/year>, which Barclays denied as fraud and is now billing to my account despite the fact that I never authorized or made this purchase. 

At the time of the transaction, I was traveling overseas and was physically outside the country, making it impossible for me to have completed this purchase myself. I did not approve this transaction, did not provide my card information for it, and had no involvement in this purchase. 

I also verified my XXXX account and there is no record of this transaction, order, or related purchase history. My credit card was never linked to my XXXX account, which strongly supports that this was an unauthorized charge and not a legitimate purchase made by me.

Since reporting this fraud, Barclays has kept my card locked, yet they continue billing me for the disputed amount. It is unreasonable to restrict my account access while simultaneously holding me financially responsible for a transaction I did not authorize.

I contacted Barclays on four separate occasions attempting to resolve this issue and was repeatedly redirected without meaningful resolution. Barclays stated they had evidence supporting the transaction ; however, the evidence provided was not accessible for proper review. The link they supplied did not function, preventing me from verifying, challenging, or responding to the alleged proof.

Barclays also issued a provisional credit during the fraud investigation and later reversed it after claiming merchant documentation supported the charge, yet they failed to provide that documentation in a usable form despite multiple requests.

Despite the lack of accessible evidence, Barclays closed the investigation, denied permanent credit, reversed the provisional credit, and held me responsible for the charge. I believe this demonstrates an inadequate investigation and failure to properly evaluate an unauthorized fraud claim.

A financial institution should not deny a fraud claim while relying on inaccessible or unverifiable evidence. The burden of proving authorization can not be met by referencing documentation the customer can not review.

I am requesting that Barclays be required to reopen this investigation, permanently reverse the fraudulent charge, remove any related interest, fees, or negative account impact, provide full accessible documentation supporting their denial, and correct the improper handling of this dispute.
